name: Lemon Chicken with Pine Nut Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 tsp grated fresh gingerroot or 1 tsp ground ginger
1/4 cup pitted and chopped Sicilian green olives
1/2 cup sweet red pepper, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
Prepare marinade: Combine olive oil, lemon, cilantro, ginger and red pepper flakes in a large plastic food storage bag. Add chicken; seal bag; shake to coat chicken evenly. Refrigerate 6 hours or overnight, turning the bag occasionally.
1. Heat oven to 375°F (190°C). Arrange chicken in a 13x9x3-inch metal baking pan. Sprinkle with salt.
2. Bake at 375°F for 45 minutes or until chicken is no longer pink near bone. Transfer to serving platter; keep warm.
3. Meanwhile, prepare Olive and Pine Nut Sauce: Pour drippings from the baking pan into a large measuring cup. Add 1/2 cup chicken broth to the baking pan; stir with a wooden spoon to loosen browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Add to drippings in the cup. Let stand until fat rises to the top.
4. Spoon 2 tbsp fat from the drippings into a medium-sized skillet. Skim remaining fat and discard. Add onion, ginger and garlic to the skillet; cook over medium heat until onion is softened and golden, about 1-2 minutes.
5. Add olives, pine nuts, pepper flakes, saffron, lemon juice, honey and remaining 1 cup chicken broth; stir until well blended. Boil until sauce is reduced and thickened, about 7 minutes. Remove from heat; swirl in butter and cilantro just until incorporated.
6. Prepare Vegetable Couscous: Heat olive oil in a medium-sized skillet. Add sweet red pepper, carrot, zucchini, red onion, turmeric and saffron. Cook over medium heat until vegetables are tender, about 8 minutes. Stir in chicken broth; bring to boiling. Remove from heat. Place couscous in a large bowl. Pour vegetable-broth mixture over couscous; toss gently to combine. Cover with plastic wrap; let stand for 15 minutes before serving.
7. Spoon Olive and Pine Nut Sauce over chicken. Serve immediately with Vegetable Couscous.
